caption.text:
Have you heard about this mass surveillance program? Flock Safety cameras are artificial intelligence automatic license plate readers, and unlike others, they’re connected to a nationwide database. 

#technology #tech #cars #auto #car #cargram #bigbrother #1984

caption.text:
They said it was for “safety.”
They said it was to “protect communities.”

Now cameras are tracking every license plate, every move, every direction you travel.

Welcome to the gray area between security and surveillance.

Flock Safety ALPR Camera is being rolled out across cities nationwide… quietly building a digital map of where people go, when they go, and how often.

Sound familiar? That’s because 1984 wasn’t supposed to be a blueprint—it was a warning.

This isn’t about fear—it’s about awareness.
At what point does protection turn into monitoring?
At what point does convenience cost freedom?

We’re not in a dystopia… but we’re definitely closer than we were yesterday.

#SurveillanceState #PrivacyMatters #DigitalFreedom #FlockCameras #1984orwell

caption.text:
FULL STORY: After failing to renew a contract with Flock safety at the previous meeting, the city sought to renegotiate the terms of the contract. At this meeting, the city had to make a final decision on the contract with the updated terms. After hours of public comment, the contract was cancelled in a 3-3 split vote. Immediately following the vote, a cop turned in her badge.
#news #breakingnews

caption.text:
🚨 Flock Safety Cameras Are on the Rise in Connecticut 🚨

These systems are appearing across neighborhoods, collecting data on vehicles and local activity. While promoted for safety, it’s worth being aware of the privacy implications behind the technology.

Knowledge is power — stay informed, stay aware, and stay ahead. 

📹 @s0phia_ops - credit

#privacy #connecticut #publicsafety #fyp #privateinvestigator

caption.text:
FULL STORY: 6/15/26 Residents of Milford, MI showed up to oppose the renewal of Flock Safety. The board unanimously voted to cancel one contract for 5 cameras. The village has a second contract for 10 cameras that will be up for renewal later this year. The council anticipates voting on the second contract in August, and in the meantime they hope to renegotiate the terms and conditions. Milford residents, please continue to email the council and ask that they cancel the remaining contract.
#news #michigan

caption.text:
Flock Safety cameras are AI-powered license plate readers quietly spreading across Texas — small black cameras on poles that scan every plate that drives by. The company has over 90,000 cameras nationwide and scans 20 billion plates per month. Around San Antonio, Texas, suburbs like Live Oak, New Braunfels, Texas, and Universal City, Texas have already installed dozens.

Audit logs have shown local police sharing Flock data with ICE for immigration enforcement — even school cameras outside Houston, Texas have been searched by out-of-state agencies for immigration leads. In Johnson County, Texas, a sheriff’s deputy searched 83,000 cameras nationwide to track a woman who self-administered an abortion — logging the search reason as “had an abortion, search for female.” They initially claimed it was a welfare check, but court records later revealed they had opened a death investigation into the fetus and consulted prosecutors about charging her.

However, communities are pushing back. Austin, Texas dumped over 540 cameras after 30+ organizations pressured the city council. San Marcos, Texas, Hays County, Texas, and Bandera, Texas all ended their Flock contracts. Across the country, people are physically ripping cameras off poles, smashing them, and spray-painting them. A Virginia man faces 25 criminal charges after destroying 13 cameras — saying he did it to defend his Fourth Amendment rights.

#texas

INJURED⁉️ | @INJURYNEEDS (1-800-888-8008)

caption.text:
“Prison can’t be the answer,” said Flock CEO Garrett Langley, when he spoke to John Collison on the Cheeky Pint podcast.

Flock is built on the belief that technology deployed responsibly can help break that cycle. Not by replacing human judgment, but by giving communities tools to deter crime before it takes root.

(cc: @johncollison)

caption.text:
What is a Flock Camera?

Flock Safety is a company that makes ALPR (Automatic License Plate Reader) cameras, which have been dispatched en masse over the past few years.

The reasons to care about mass surveillance are plentiful, but today we’ll simply be talking about a few tools to help you be more aware of surveillance capabilities in your area.

deflock.me is a crowdsourced site that tracks the location of Flocks and other ALPR cameras. We’d highly recommend visiting their site and looking at locations around your AO. As with all crowdsourced data, make sure to validate it; @christopher_m_rance has a good video on using Google street view for this.

For mapping out locations for internal use or to contribute to deflock, ATAK, CalTopo, OsmAnd, or any other mapping tool is more than suitable. @getgotak and @guerrilladynamics have put together a datapack for ATAK based on the deflock data; this is the easiest way to get current ALPR locations.

Most ALPRs will first detect the motion of a vehicle before recording a series of images, which makes them incredibly easy to spot under night vision.

In this video, we’re using a cheap android phone and our covert EUD mount to run ATAK from a fanny pack.
caption.text_translation:
What is a Flock Camera? 

 Flock Safety is a company that makes ALPR (Automatic License Plate Reader) cameras, which have been dispatched en masse over the past few years. 

 The reasons to care about mass surveillance are plentiful, but today we’ll simply be talking about a few tools to help you be more aware of surveillance capabilities in your area. 

 deflock.me is a crowdsourced site that tracks the location of Flocks and other ALPR cameras. We’d highly recommend visiting their site and looking at locations around your AO. As with all crowdsourced data, make sure to validate it; @christopher_m_rance has a good video on using Google street view for this. 

 For mapping out locations for internal use or to contribute to deflock, ATAK, CalTopo, OsmAnd, or any other mapping tool is more than suitable. @getgotak and @guerrilladynamics have put together a datapack for ATAK based on the deflock data; this is the easiest way to get current ALPR locations. 

 Most ALPRs will first detect the motion of a vehicle before recording a series of images, which makes them incredibly easy to spot under night vision. 

 In this video, we’re using a cheap android phone and our covert EUD mount to run ATAK from a fanny pack.

caption.text:
FULL STORY: A crowd of people showed up last night to oppose Flock safety drones, and the board treated us as a nuisance and a problem to be solved.

After starting the meeting almost an hour late, the board voted to move all public comment to the end of the meeting and proceeded to vote to approve Flock Drones without listening to a single word from any us.

This footage was captured during a break right before they voted.

They didn’t just ignore us, they sneered and laughed at our outrage over their abuse of power.

The drones were approved by chairman David Woodward (D), Penny Luebs (D), Michael Spisz (R), Michael Gingell (R), Bob Hoffman (R), Karen Joliat (R), Christine Long (R), Philip Weipert (R), Robert Smiley (R), William Miller (D), Linnie Taylor (D), Marcia Gershenson (D), Angela Powell (D)- who proposed moving public comment to the end👀, Gwen Markham (D), and Yolanda Charles (D), and, absent from the meeting, Brendan Johnson (D)
They will not be up for reelection until 2028.

As a result, we will be fighting to recall David Woodward. This is NOT the first time this has happened.
The recall requires wet signatures from registered voters in Oakland County. Please send me your email if you are willing to sign.

If you can’t sign, you can still help! We need to move fast to organize and make this happen, reach out to me if you want to join the group chat to get involved.

This meeting ended just before midnight because we all stayed to voice our opinions anyway. Thank you to everyone who showed up, and I was inspired by each and every one of you.

#news #breakingnews #michigan

caption.text: A woman just got a $1,250 ticket in the mail because a Flock Safety camera said she was on her phone while driving. The problem is her phone was face down on her lap and both hands were on the steering wheel. These AI cameras are out here writing tickets for things that did not even happen. This is what happens when technology replaces common sense.
